content.claim: Central European University analysis and research on primary-level segregation attribute Hungary's extreme between-school stratification primarily to structural factors (neighborhood segregation, long-standing admission policies, school choice mechanisms) rather than to the 6/8-year track alone, and caution that educational policymakers may need to be 'much more radical' structural interventions to foster integration.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Polish system reform lens
limitations: Source material provided in notes is truncated ('characteristics of schools than to their social [composition alone]')
This is an interpretive synthesis rather than direct empirical isolation of causal mechanisms
The claim about what policymakers 'may need' is predictive rather than descriptive
No quantified decomposition of variance attributable to track structure vs. other mechanisms is provided
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
